disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent collapse/near-collapse occurring reproducibly within 60-90 minutes of a large midday meal, three episodes in five days
trigger_question_examples: When exactly do the collapses happen?
Is there any pattern to when she falls?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's always after her lunch — usually within an hour or so of a big meal. She's gone down three times this week, always after eating.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lightheadedness, visual disturbance and legs giving way specifically on standing after meals
trigger_question_examples: What does she feel just before she goes down?
Does it happen when she stands up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She says the room spins and she sees stars, and her legs give out when she tries to stand up after eating.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, palpitations or breathlessness reported before the collapses
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or racing heart before she faints?
Is she short of breath beforehand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, she doesn't complain of chest pain or a racing heart or being short of breath before it happens — she just suddenly goes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known longstanding Parkinson's disease and a morning antihypertensive tablet
trigger_question_examples: Does she have any medical conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's had Parkinson's for years, and she takes a blood pressure tablet every morning plus her Parkinson's medication.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound post-meal fatigue and drowsiness with slumping immediately after eating
trigger_question_examples: How is she straight after eating?
Does she get very tired after meals?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Right after she eats she gets overwhelmingly sleepy and slumps in her chair, then a bit later comes the dizziness when we move her.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood in vomit or stool observed; no melaena reported by staff
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone noticed any blood in her vomit or bowel motions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, we haven't seen any blood or black motions that we're aware of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High risk of traumatic head injury or fracture from unpredictable recurrent syncope
trigger_question_examples: Has she hurt herself in any of the falls?
How are you keeping her safe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: So far she hasn't hit her head, but we're terrified she will — that's why we've had to keep her in a wheelchair after meals.
